renderingPipeline.description: How DD2 composes character sprites from tile data
steps: 1. Character state machine (offset 27 of entity) selects animation state
2. Animation state handler ($C8D0 table) initializes movement/frame params
3. Frame update ($D7FA) copies 12-byte animation record to $0BFB buffer
4. Bank-switched call ($FB00 -> $4000 -> $43D4) steps animation frame counter
5. When frame counter expires, animation step routine loads next tile set
6. Character rendering ($C7D4/$C822 tables) calls sprite draw routine
7. Sprite draw ($4467 in banked ROM) writes tile pairs to sprite buffer
8. Tile pair encoding: [H, L] -> tile_index=(H<<4)|(L>>4), palette=L&0x0F
9. Buffer offset computed from sprite type ($44EB) + set_index*16 + sprite_index
10. DMA routine ($44F1) transfers buffer to actual sprite RAM ($1000-$17FF)

default.description: 12-byte default animation record copied to $0BFB for normal states

specialState9.description: 12-byte lookup table for special state 9 (ride/jump) indexed by entity type

format.byte0_1: Pointer to sprite RAM position (16-bit address)
format.byte2: Tile data byte 1 (written to sprite RAM at pointer)
format.byte3: Tile data byte 2 (written to sprite RAM at pointer + 512)

singleSpriteBuffer.description: Used for single-tile sprites (projectiles, effects, small objects)

format.byte0_1: Pointer to sprite RAM base position (16-bit address)
format.byte2_33: 16 pairs of [tile_data_1, tile_data_2] for 16 sprite tiles

multiSpriteBuffer.description: Used for multi-tile characters (2x3 or 3x3 grid of 16x16 tiles)

tileEncoding.description: Tile data is encoded as 2 bytes [H, L]

spriteRAMLayout.byteAt_ptr: Y position (or tile high byte, depending on exact hardware)
spriteRAMLayout.note: The exact mapping between buffer bytes and sprite RAM positions depends on the DD2 custom hardware sprite chip. The DMA routine writes byte pairs at offset 0 and offset 512 from the base pointer.

animStateMap.description: Maps input/state to animation sub-state index

tileOffsetMap.description: Maps input/state to base tile offset added to sprite tile byte

subStateDispatch.description: Maps speed level to animation handler index

characterTileRanges.description: Tile high-byte references found in character rendering code regions
